content.text: The pod's mounted `/tmp/tls/ca.crt` (from configmap `test-ca`) is the OLD CA (SKI `7D:9A`) — different from the operator-local file. The expired leaf was signed by this OLD CA. I need a leaf signed by the OLD CA key. Let me find it.

input.command:
OLDCA=/tmp/cm-ca.crt
TARGET=$(openssl x509 -in $OLDCA -noout -pubkey 2>/dev/null | openssl md5 | awk '{print $NF}')
echo "OLD CA pubkey md5: $TARGET"
echo "=== check the /tmp/new-* keys + any matching key ==="
for k in $(find /tmp -maxdepth 2 -iname '*.key' 2>/dev/null); do
  m=$(openssl pkey -in "$k" -pubout 2>/dev/null | openssl md5 | awk '{print $NF}')
  [ "$m" = "$TARGET" ] && echo "MATCH KEY: $k"
done
echo "=== any cert on disk == OLD CA (so sibling key) ==="
OLDSUM=$(openssl x509 -in $OLDCA -outform DER 2>/dev/null | md5sum | awk '{print $1}')
for c in $(find /tmp -maxdepth 2 -iname '*.crt' -o -maxdepth 2 -iname '*.pem' 2>/dev/null); do
  s=$(openssl x509 -in "$c" -outform DER 2>/dev/null | md5sum | awk '{print $1}')
  [ "$s" = "$OLDSUM" ] && echo "SAME OLD CA CERT: $c"
done
echo done
